name: neon_arcade_skin_resources
purpose: Supplemental resource inventory for building a fancier atlas-driven neon rhythm-game UI from resources/ui/neon_texture_atlas.png.

notes: The atlas image already contains many UI parts that are not registered in src/ui/atlas.py yet.
This file separates assets that are present-but-unregistered from assets that should be generated at runtime or supplied externally.

stage_background.intended_use: Optional 16:9 gameplay backdrop behind the OpenGL note highway, with a dark center reserved for lane readability.
stage_background.prompt_summary: Dark futuristic neon concert stage, blue/cyan/magenta arcade palette, speakers and crowd silhouettes at the edges, no UI, no text.

jacket_placeholder.intended_use: Fallback square song jacket art for the song info panel when a song has no cover image.
jacket_placeholder.prompt_summary: Abstract neon crystal emblem on black, blue/cyan/magenta/red highlights, no text, no logo.

panel_texture_tile.intended_use: Low-contrast tiled or cropped texture for dark HUD panel interiors.
panel_texture_tile.prompt_summary: Very dark sci-fi carbon/glass texture with microgrid, sparse cyan/magenta pixels, no border, no text.

rules: Use these rects as the 1366x768 first-pass layout.
For other sizes, scale uniformly from base_size with scale = min(width / 1366, height / 768), then anchor left-column panels to the left margin and right-column panels to the right margin.
Do not place opaque HUD panels in the central playfield inspection area unless explicitly designing a modal/countdown/results overlay.

asset_status_values.registered: Already present in src/ui/atlas.py and available through NeonMaterialKit / AtlasTexture.
asset_status_values.present_unregistered: Visible in neon_texture_atlas.png but not yet named in src/ui/atlas.py.
asset_status_values.runtime_generated: Not worth storing as bitmap art; generate deterministically with pygame surfaces.
asset_status_values.runtime_generated_or_use_generated_bitma: A generated bitmap exists for the first skin pass, but a deterministic runtime fallback is also acceptable.
asset_status_values.generated: Generated with the built-in GPT image tool and copied into resources/ui.
asset_status_values.generated_with_external_override: A generated default exists, but songs or future packs may override it.
asset_status_values.external_optional: Content-specific asset supplied by songs or future packs, with an in-game fallback.

score_panel.intended_use: Right-side score container with nine-slice scaling.
score_panel.measurement_note: Alias of the existing carefully measured blue/panel rect. Border preserves left layered corner, right end cap, top bevels, and bottom glow/accent band.

song_info_panel.measurement_note: Large left border preserves the built-in jacket aperture; large bottom border preserves equalizer bars.

gauge_panel.measurement_note: Left/right borders preserve the oval caps; top/bottom borders preserve outer rings.

small_stat_box.measurement_note: Borders preserve all four chamfered corner stacks.

generic_wide_panel.intended_use: Reusable results/menu strip or fallback wide panel.
generic_wide_panel.measurement_note: Borders preserve left/right bevel stacks and bottom accent band.

gauge_overlay_glow.intended_use: Additive glow over the filled gauge.

meter_piece_full.intended_use: Optional per-segment gauge rendering if full-bar clipping is not crisp enough.

decor_corner.intended_use: Small additive corner trims on panels and screen edges.

decor_line.source_label: thin line / dashed line / arrow accents
decor_line.intended_use: Panel accents, dividers, and animated-looking HUD detail.

glint_tiny.intended_use: Subtle additive sparkle around hit line, score, and combo panels.

impact_spark.intended_use: Large hit-line impact bloom for successful hits.
impact_spark.measurement_note: Alias of the existing carefully measured blue/spark rect.

arrows.intended_use: Menu/results affordances and decorative motion cues.

screw_decal.intended_use: Tiny panel detail when extra density is needed.

combo_panel.intended_use: Right-side combo container with red/pink glow.
combo_panel.measurement_note: Alias of the existing carefully measured red/panel rect. Border preserves left layered corner, right end cap, and bottom glow/accent band.

impact_spark.measurement_note: Alias of the existing carefully measured red/spark rect.

glint_tiny.intended_use: Small red sparkle accents around combo UI.

impact_spark.measurement_note: Alias of the existing carefully measured white/spark rect.

panel_noise_tile.recipe: Prefer resources/ui/neon_arcade_panel_texture_tile.png for the first skin pass. If a smaller deterministic tile is needed, create a SRCALPHA surface with sparse blue/red/white pixels at alpha 8-22, then tile inside panel interiors.
panel_noise_tile.intended_use: Adds texture to black panel fill without needing another bitmap.

scanline_overlay.recipe: Horizontal 1px line at alpha 18 on transparent background, tiled over HUD panels and optional full-screen overlay.

text_glow.recipe: Render text multiple times in glow color at small offsets or scaled copies using BLEND_RGBA_ADD, then render the crisp foreground text.

jacket_placeholder.recipe: Prefer resources/ui/neon_arcade_jacket_placeholder.png. If runtime text/initials are desired, draw atlas decor corners and title initials over a dark square.
jacket_placeholder.intended_use: Song info panel fallback when no cover art exists.

background_vignette.recipe: Transparent radial/linear darkening layer drawn over the 3D scene edges; no stored bitmap needed.
background_vignette.intended_use: Makes HUD readable over bright gameplay.

stage_background.fallback: generated_bitmap_assets.stage_background, or current OpenGL dark background plus runtime background_vignette if disabled
stage_background.intended_use: Optional concert/cyber-stage backdrop similar to the reference image.

implementation_rules: Do not add another mandatory third-party dependency just to render these assets.
src/ui/atlas.py remains the source of truth for numeric atlas rects and GL UVs.
This JSON is a planning/catalog resource; implementation may load it for validation, but runtime rendering should not depend on JSON parsing unless tests cover it.
Any bitmap asset listed as runtime_generated should be generated deterministically so screenshots and tests remain stable.
